forecasts.reasoning:
The question asks if there will be at least one day in a 42-day window (May 20 to June 30) where the word "tariff(s)" is absent from BOTH the NYT Today's Paper digital section and the WSJ print front page URL. To resolve NO, at least one of these two pages must feature the exact word "tariff" or "tariffs" in its dynamic text (headlines, blurbs, A1 articles, etc.) every single day without fail for nearly six weeks. 

While tariffs are the dominant economic story, maintaining a 42-day unbroken streak of a specific word across limited screen/page real estate is extremely difficult. The WSJ front page (A1 and What's News) has limited space and features diverse topics (geopolitics, AI, corporate mergers, other domestic policies, SCOTUS rulings in late June). More importantly, WSJ does not publish a Sunday paper, meaning that on all six Sundays in this window, WSJ's URL will likely 404 or return empty, meaning it contributes 0 mentions. On those six Sundays, the streak completely depends on the NYT Today's Paper index explicitly using the word "tariff" rather than synonyms like "trade war," "duties," or focusing on non-trade news. Additionally, holiday weekends like Memorial Day often see a slowdown in business news and markets. The probability of breaking this streak and getting at least ONE day without a mention on either page (which results in a YES) is very high.
